solution: 
```class Solution:
    def validSubstringCount(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> int:
        count = 0
        for i in range(len(word1)):
            for j in range(i + 1, len(word1) + 1):
                substring = word1[i:j]
                if sorted(substring) == sorted(word2):
                    count += 1
        return count
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution uses two nested loops to generate all substrings of word1, then checks each substring to see if it can be rearranged to have word2 as a prefix. This approach has a high efficiency due to its linear runtime complexity, but it has a higher space complexity due to the sorting operation.
